summary: The capsule correctly traces memory-pressure warnings through OOM kills, 502/503 cascades, and stale-cache exhaustion, but the end-of-incident state (whether the recommendations service recovered) is absent, leaving the engineer without a resolution timestamp.
improvements: Add a recovery or final-state evidence entry (e.g. the last observed 503 or any restart/recovery line if present) so the window close is meaningful
Label 'recommendations service oom killed' (line 237) as root_cause rather than trigger, since OOM kill is the proximate cause of all downstream 502s
